text:
Alice had the most vivid dreams all summer. She dreamt she was a famous mystery solver. She dressed up like Cleopatra—scarlet gown. Whenever she was sleepy, she'd sneak into her grandmother's bookshelf just to hear the mysteries unopened.

One night the bookshelf fell over. She groaned. The only thing that felt real in her dream was holding a magnifying glass.

She picked the book up.

"This place doesn't belong to me."

On the third page she saw her grandmother's signature. A note said, "Always remember our attic is where we belong."

Alice snored. She'd sleep like that until dawn.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
